title: Switzerland to collaborate with J&K on sustainable tourism, vocational training
content: Switzerland’s Ambassador to India announced that the country “will continue to work together in the region, from a Himalayan glaciology collaborative program at the University of Kashmir to cloud research with ETH Zurich”

title: Indian court ruling on Google keyword ads could reshape online advertising
content: The court ordered Google to pay ‌damages of $31,600 in a ruling issued on May 22 by the ‌Delhi High Court

title: Himachal panchayat elections: Over 19% polling recorded till 9 a.m. in final phase
content: The Sirmaur district had the highest voter turnout at 23.60%, while Lahaul and Spiti registered the lowest at 16.23%

title: K.P. Dhanapalan, senior Congress leader and former Kerala MP, passes away
content: A prominent face of the Congress in Ernakulam, Mr. Dhanapalan was elected to the Lok Sabha from Chalakudy constituency in 2009

title: 89 additional CAPF units, advanced surveillance, real-time monitoring for safe Amarnath yatra this year
content: Police officials said J&K Director General of Police Nalin Prabhat directed the district heads “to ensure proper arrangements for incoming units and their efficient ground deployment”

title: Man killed in wild elephant attack in Kerala’s Athirappilly; protest hartal called
content: 65-year-old attacked near his home in Vaisyeri; locals demand urgent action as human-wildlife conflict intensifies

title: T.U. Kuruvilla, former Kerala PWD Minister, dies
content: An active member of the Kerala Congress since its formative years, he was part for the V.S. Achuthanandan Cabinet in 2006

title: Fire engulfs Hyderabad-Kakinada private bus on NH-65; 36 passengers escape
content: The bus, operated by Rainbow Travels, had departed from Miyapur in Hyderabad and was en route to Kakinada when a suspected short circuit triggered a fire in the front portion of the vehicle

title: Destruction of rocks at Defence land in Ammuguda decried
content: Ammuguda hillocks are home to ancient rock formations and open scrub lands which are used by the society members and local residents for trekking, and rock climbing activities

title: Senior citizen’s caretaker, accomplice arrested on charge of robbing gold and valuables in Belagavi
content: The caretaker allegedly stole the valuables in small portions, and deposited them in a private firm in exchange for gold loans

title: Manoj Jarange Patil firm to launch indefinite hunger strike from May 30
content: Manoj Jarange Patil dismissed the claims of 58 lakhs Kunbi entries being a misunderstanding by BJP Minister Radhakrishna Vikhe Patil, saying that these figures were announced by the government itself

title: Tiger ‘Explorer’ walks back to Papikonda National Park
content: It was inching closer to Yeleru reservoir from the Pampa reservoir through dense forest cover in the Kakinada district by Tuesday night.

title: Maharastra tiger Explorer returns to East Godavari again
content: The tiger has crossed the Godavari River twice after it was released into Papikonda National Park on February 16, say officials

title: Kolhapur forms team of 10 transwomen for disaster management and rescue work
content: The disaster management cell of the State government has claimed that it is the first such initiative in Maharashtra

title: Spurious liquor kills 15 in Pune, Pimpri-Chinchwad; 8 held, probe ordered
content: Maharashtra CM orders police commissioners to conduct a joint probe; raids conducted at multiple liquor shops overnight; Opposition leaders allege failure in law enforcement, accuse police of a cover-up

title: Portion of platform shed collapses at Lucknow station, three injured
content: Sources said renovation and concourse construction work was underway at the station and portions of the platform number five shed had already been dismantled. The collapsed section was also slated for removal

title: Worth examining Vijay’s appeal to children to convince adults to vote for him, says judge citing personal anecdote
content: He opines that a case filed against TVK for making an appeal to children during elections is worth being looked into

title: AAP wins most wards in Punjab local polls, Opposition alleges misuse of govt. machinery
content: Punjab Chief Minister Bhagwant Mann says AAP’s victory in the municipal elections is a decisive endorsement of his government’s development-driven governance model

title: Kerala’s Pariyathukavu residents hope government intervention will put an end to eviction threat
content: Pariyathukavu, a settlement in Ernakulam district, occupied by seven Dalit families for decades, has become the epicentre of a socio-political controversy with attempts to evict the residents, who have been engaged in a prolonged legal battle to protect their homes. G. Ragesh visits the area to gauge the gravity of the situation as both the residents and the claimants of the land await anxiously for finality

title: Sharp curves, unfinished roadwork identified as factors behind 320 crashes and 251 deaths at Cyberabad’s 43 black spots
content: Over 90% of the identified accident-prone stretches fall under the Medchal zone; NH-65 stretch emerged as the deadliest corridor with over 50% black spots.

title: Hectic parleys in Delhi over Ministry formation; CLP to meet today
content: Governor Thaawarchand Gehlot, on May 29, 2026, accepted the resignation of Chief Minister Siddaramaiah and asked him to continue as the caretaker chief minister till the next government is formed

title: Over 90% of Indian babies born in hospitals, 87% of one-year-olds fully vaccinated: NFHS-6
content: Survey conducted in 2023-2024 found reduced stunting, severe wasting among young children, improvements in services and nutrition for pregnant women, progress towards universal vaccination

title: Supreme Court issues guidelines for protecting survivors of human trafficking
content: The petition highlighting the lack of laws and protective mechanisms for young girls trafficked into prostitution was filed by the non-governmental organisation (NGO) Prajwala in 2004

title: Respect past agreements, initiate dialogue in Kashmir: RJD parliamentarian Sudhakar Singh
content: “Unfortunately, those who talk about human rights and democratic rights are being jailed,” Sudhakar Singh, referring to the incarceration of MP Rashid, said

title: Senior IPS officer Mahesh Kumar Aggarwal appointed Head of the Tamil Nadu Police
content: The 1994-Batch officer is serving as the DGP in the Border Security Force, Delhi. He replaces DGP/HoPF Sandeep Rai  Rathore. He was in the panel of three IPS officers recommended by the Union Public Service Commission as fit for appointment to the top post

title: West Bengal government faces Annapurna challenge, to verify beneficiaries before rolling out cash transfer scheme
content: The financial burden which Annapurna Bhandar would have on the State exchequer is what is prompting the West Bengal government to verify the existing beneficiaries of Lakshmir Bhandar and drag its feet on the implementation of the scheme

title: West Bengal CM meets Darjeeling hill leaders, acts tough on GTA corruption
content: Chief Minister Suvendu Adhikari said that the West Bengal government will seek benefits Pradhan Mantri Cha Shramik Protsahan Yojana (PMCSPY) which the previous government did not subscribe to.

title: Uttar Pradesh emphasised to prevent water-borne diseases in rural areas through community driven participation
content: Mr. Shekhar, a 2004 batch Indian Administrative Service (IAS) officer, added that the women associated with Field Testing work are being paid honorarium according to the prescribed rates for the testing work carried out by them

title: Congress holds talks with Hemant Soren on fielding party nominee from one of 2 Rajya Sabha seats going to polls in Jharkhand
content: One seat became vacant following the death of former Jharkhand Mukti Morcha president and former CM Shibu Soren last year, while the second will fall vacant on June 21 when the term of BJP leader Deepak Prakash ends

title: PM’s silence on CBSE OSM controversy shows he cares more about his govt’s survival than students’ future: Rahul Gandhi
content: Opposition leader questions how TCS lost CBSE contract to COEMPT, which had a poor past track record.

title: Supreme Court sets aside 'fraud' findings, disgorgement of ₹447 crore in 2007 RPL Futures Trading case
content: The court said there was no evidence that Reliance had any fraudulent intent or was attempting to corner the market merely because it held 40.10% open interest

title: Supreme Court sets 3-month deadline for High Courts to pronounce judgments after reserving orders
content: The Bench held that the bail orders must be ideally pronounced the next day, and communicated to the jail the same day

title: Delhi High Court upholds TRAI regulations capping television ads to 12 minutes per hour
content: Court says broadcasters cannot claim an ‘unfettered right to exploit spectrum for commercial purposes’, adding that ‘excessive or uneven commercial intrusion is a direct impairment of the right of consumers to a fair and reasonable viewing experience’

title: Karnataka has given the Congress a template to settle leadership feuds
content: Congress leaders Mallikarjun Kharge and Rahul Gandhi’s success in persuading Siddaramaiah to step down may now provide the party a template to handle similar leadership disputes

title: Naga body threatens to boycott Manipur government
content: The All Naga Students’ Association, Manipur also sets a similar timeline for the abrogation of ceasefire deals with Kuki extremist groups

title: Greens seek curb on unregulated tourism in Wayanad
content: The Wayanad Prakrithi Samrakshana Samiti alleges that unregulated tourism continues unabated in the mountain ranges even after the devastating Puthumala landslide in 2024

title: Athletes hit hard as sports medicine institute remains shut
content: ‘The institute has equipment worth over ₹2 crore for sports injury treatment that is deteriorating due to prolonged disuse’

title: Kerala start-up unveils first AI system-on-chip
content: The chip has successfully completed silicon bring-up, a critical stage of laboratory testing, and is being readied for commercial production, says the company

title: Action initiated against five temple staff for demanding money from devotees in Tiruchendur
content: The move follows a surprise inspection by HR&CE S. Ramesh at the Subramanya Swami Temple, during which several irregularities in darshan arrangements and other processes were identified

title: Dedicated policy framework proposed for Sivakasi’s ₹6,000-crore fireworks industry
content: Minister for Industries S. Keerthana says the reform aims to further formalise the sector through specific and targeted measures that address multiple needs of all stakeholders

title: Residents block Arakkonam-Thakkolam Main Road in Ranipet against power outage
content: Unscheduled power cuts are affecting household appliances, and irrigation activities in farmlands amid cropping season, say residents of Parameswaramangalam village

title: Former AIADMK MLA from Jayamkondam joins TVK
content: In a related development, the AIADMK’s S. Ravi, a former Arakkonam MLA, says he has met TVK leader and Water Resources Minister N. Anand and has sought an appointment to join the party

title: Woman ‘murdered’ by paramour in Eluru; body exhumed
content: Accused, Darshan Francis, allegedly killed Vishali on April 18, burnt her clothes and buried the body suspecting that she was speaking with others over phone, Eluru DSP D. Sravan Kumar

title: From Ahinda to Mekedatu: 5 challenges before Karnataka's new CM
content: Karnataka's new Congress government, led by DK Shivakumar, faces a critical transition. Key challenges include cabinet formation balancing caste and regional demands, sustaining welfare guarantees amid fiscal strain, and navigating the sensitive Mekedatu reservoir dispute with Tamil Nadu. Shivakumar must also retain the crucial Ahinda support base and ensure development extends beyond Bengaluru to rural areas.

title: Is Indus Treaty deadlock hurting Pak? Karachi reels under chronic water shortage
content: Karachi faces a severe water crisis during peak summer, coinciding with Eid-ul-Adha. Jamaat-e-Islami Pakistan chief Hafiz Naeem ur Rehman has accused the ruling PPP of gross mismanagement and failure to address the city's chronic water shortages despite 18 years in power.  Nearly 70% of the city is reportedly experiencing extended supply disruptions, forcing residents to buy expensive private tankers.

title: 'If namaz on road wrong, ban all festivals': Owaisi points at 'double standard'
content: AIMIM chief Asaduddin Owaisi criticized what he termed a double standard regarding religious practices, stating that if offering namaz on roads is deemed wrong, then restrictions should apply equally to all faiths, citing Article 25 of the Constitution. He questioned why objections are raised against Muslim prayers but not religious processions of other communities.

title: 'They walk among us': White House launches alien-themed website to track immigrants
content: The White House launched a new space-themed website, Aliens.gov, to highlight immigration enforcement actions. Using extraterrestrial imagery, the site aims to showcase arrests of undocumented immigrants and promote President Trump's agenda. It features a live dashboard and interactive map of enforcement activities, drawing criticism for its dehumanizing language.

title: Microsoft CEO's Copilot ‘confusion fix’ that left employees laughing in townhall
content: Microsoft is reportedly developing a 'super app' to consolidate its confusing array of Copilot AI tools. This unified platform aims to end customer frustration by offering a single destination for various AI assistants, including coding, chat, and collaboration features. The app, expected by summer's end, will allow seamless switching between personal and work accounts, simplifying Microsoft's AI ecosystem.

title: The Rs 7 cr bargain vs the Rs 25 cr gamble: How Holder outshone IPL 2026's biggest buys
content: IPL auctions are often won on perception in December, but the real story usually emerges months later. While franchises splashed massive sums on Cameron Green, Matheesha Pathirana and Liam Livingstone, the smartest buys of IPL 2026 quietly came from the Rs 7-9 crore bracket. Jason Holder topped the charts by a distance, Josh Inglis maximised limited opportunities, and Ravi Bishnoi delivered steady returns. Meanwhile, several marquee signings struggled with injuries, poor form and role mismatches, turning auction-night hype into underwhelming seasons.

title: Rain havoc in Bengaluru: Man killed after wall crashes on auto
content: A Bengaluru autorickshaw driver died tragically when a private club's compound wall collapsed on his parked vehicle during heavy rain and strong winds. Shivaboraiah was trapped and crushed by the debris. Police are investigating the cause of the wall's collapse.

title: CNG price hiked to Rs 86 per kg in Mumbai, piped gas to Rs 52 per unit
content: Compressed Natural Gas (CNG) prices have increased by Rs 2 per kg in the Mumbai Metropolitan Region, taking the new rate to Rs 86 per kg. Piped cooking gas will also see a 50 paise per unit hike. This rise is attributed to global energy supply chain disruptions and increased procurement costs.

title: ‘Students are struggling’: California University professors press for return of standardised exams
content: University of California faculty are urging the reinstatement of SAT/ACT math requirements for STEM applicants, citing a significant decline in student math skills since the tests were dropped. They argue current admissions practices fail to ensure readiness, forcing instructors to reteach basic math and jeopardizing the quality of STEM education.

title: Nagpur man slips, dies in house; bedridden wife starves to death amid extreme heat
content: A 77-year-old retired defence employee and his bedridden wife were found dead in their locked Nagpur home. The husband likely died first after a fall, leaving his wife to succumb days later to dehydration and starvation in the extreme summer heat. Their tragic end highlights a painful and lonely death due to lack of immediate support.

title: Mumbai Indians concerned about numerous dressing room 'leaks'
content: Mumbai Indians faced a dismal IPL 2026, plagued by persistent dressing room unrest and damaging leaks of sensitive information. This internal turmoil, escalating since Hardik Pandya's captaincy, has frustrated franchise figures and insiders alike. Management is now prioritising an immediate crackdown on these breaches to restore the team's former glory.

title: Gujarat-origin man pleads guilty in $9.5m gambling racket in US
content: A Gujarat-origin man residing in the US has pleaded guilty to a multimillion-dollar illegal gambling conspiracy in southwest Missouri. Tushar Patel admitted his role in a racket that generated nearly $9.5 million in illegal proceeds. He faces charges including conspiracy to commit wire fraud, operate an illegal gambling business, and money laundering.

title: American billionaire Mark Cuban sees ‘deep trouble’ for Sam Altman’s OpenAI
content: Shark Tank investor Mark Cuban warns OpenAI's massive fundraising may lead to 'deep trouble' if economics don't deliver, questioning AI infrastructure spending returns. He also expressed uncertainty about the LLM market's future and criticized AI firms for hyping risks. Separately, Cuban revealed selling most Bitcoin, finding it an unreliable hedge against global turmoil.

title: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi breaks down in dugout after Rajasthan Royals' IPL 2026 exit
content: Fifteen-year-old Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's IPL 2026 dream ended in tears despite his stellar 96, as Gujarat Titans advanced to the final.  The young Rajasthan Royals star, who amassed 776 runs this season, was visibly heartbroken after his team's elimination.  Captain Riyan Parag lauded his exceptional talent and maturity, predicting a bright future for the prodigious cricketer.

title: Gulf's other war: UAE carried out strikes on Iran despite ceasefire — Report
content: The UAE's involvement in the Middle East conflict was deeper than previously known, with coordinated strikes on Iran, including energy facilities, even after an April ceasefire. These actions exposed divisions within the Gulf, as Saudi Arabia expressed concerns about escalating risks to regional energy supplies and global markets.

title: Ranveer Singh 'Don 3' row: Ram Gopal Varma questions FWICE action, says ‘it’s a kangaroo court’
content: Filmmaker Ram Gopal Varma has publicly supported Ranveer Singh amidst the 'Don 3' controversy, criticizing the FWICE's non-cooperation directive. Varma argued that the FWICE lacks authority and that stars like Singh are crucial to the industry's existence and employment, not the federation.

title: Sikh man’s murder conviction in UK triggers storm over carrying kirpan in public
content: A British Sikh man was convicted of murder using a kirpan, sparking calls to ban the ceremonial dagger in public. Accusations of two-tier policing arose after officers handcuffed the dying victim, believing a false claim of racial abuse. The victim's mother was convicted of aiding the killer. Investigations into police conduct are underway.

title: Tamannaah Bhatia's old temple video sparks attention
content: Actress Tamannaah Bhatia's recent temple visit in Mumbai has gone viral, showcasing her spiritual side. Previously, she shared how Isha Yoga Centre practices, particularly meditation, have profoundly impacted her life, leading to increased joy and purpose. Tamannaah emphasizes spirituality's role in achieving happiness independent of external circumstances, encouraging others to explore its benefits.

title: 'Poison gas, wear mask, no water': Man dies by suicide, leaves poignant note behind
content: A 42-year-old man, struggling with unemployment and financial distress, allegedly died by suicide in Gandhinagar. He left a note warning his family about poisonous substances in the house, showing concern for his daughter's safety. Police suspect the death occurred around May 22, with toxic pesticide packets recovered from the scene.

title: Four lines that 'layoff email' from Meta Leadership has for those on H-1B visa
content: Meta has initiated another round of significant layoffs, impacting nearly 8,000 employees globally, with notifications starting in Singapore. While many are let go, over 7,000 will transition to AI initiatives. The company is providing immigration guidance and severance packages, including 16 weeks' pay plus two weeks per service year, and 18 months of COBRA coverage for affected staff.

title: Trump will accept only Iran deal that meets his red lines: White House
content: President Trump has yet to approve a proposed deal with Iran, emphasizing that any agreement must meet his conditions, including preventing Iran from acquiring nuclear weapons. While discussions continue, Iran denies negotiating under US dictates. The uncertainty impacts energy markets, with regional tensions also escalating between Iran, the US, and Israel-Hezbollah.

title: Karnataka: CLP to elect DK Shivakumar leader today, paving way for CM post
content: DK Shivakumar is set to be formally elected as the new leader of the Congress Legislature Party in Bengaluru, succeeding Chief Minister Siddaramaiah. AICC leaders will attend the meeting where Shivakumar will be elected, followed by his submission of the CLP resolution to the governor to stake his claim in forming the new cabinet.

title: US Judge orders removal of Trump's name from Kennedy Center; president fumes
content: A US federal judge has ordered the Trump administration to remove President Trump's name from the Kennedy Center. The court ruled that renaming the Washington institution, a memorial to John F. Kennedy, requires congressional approval. The judge mandated the removal of all Trump signage and references within two weeks, following a board decision that faced significant backlash.

title: Shubman on his haunches, Shastri asks 'Where's the fevicol?' after Sudharsan's bizarre wicket
content: Gujarat Titans batter Sai Sudharsan experienced a rare hit-wicket dismissal twice in consecutive IPL matches.  This unusual event occurred during Qualifier 2 against Rajasthan Royals.  Despite this setback, Gujarat Titans secured a seven-wicket victory.  Skipper Shubman Gill expressed surprise at the repeated dismissal.  Gujarat Titans will now face Royal Challengers Bengaluru in the final.

title: 'Drishyam 3' BO day 9: Mohanlal's film slows at Rs 4.30 cr
content: Mohanlal's 'Drishyam 3' has achieved a remarkable Rs 200 crore worldwide, driven by strong overseas performance. While domestic collections remain solid at Rs 86.25 crore net, a noticeable dip in footfalls was observed on Day 9. Occupancy trends indicate a mid-week slowdown, though Kerala continues to be the film's strongest market.

title: 'More than ready': Sangakkara backs Vaibhav Sooryavanshi for India call-up after dream IPL
content: Kumar Sangakkara believes 15-year-old Vaibhav Sooryavanshi is ready for international cricket after a remarkable IPL campaign. The Rajasthan Royals opener amassed 776 runs in 16 innings at a blistering strike rate of 237.30, including one century and five fifties. The teenager could have had back-to-back centuries in the playoffs, but fell short on 97 and 96, respectively.

title: Sonakshi Sinha rents out Bandra luxury home to Kuwait Consulate for Rs 1.92 crore: Report
content: Sonakshi Sinha has leased out her upscale apartment in Mumbai’s Bandra West to the Consulate General of the State of Kuwait for a yearly rent of Rs 1.92 crore, property registration documents accessed by CRE Matrix revealed. The apartment has reportedly been rented for the residential use of Kuwait’s Consul General Emad Abdul Aziz Al-Kharaz along with his family members and staff. The agreement states that the monthly rent for the property is Rs 16 lakh. The lease period begins on June 7, 2026, and will continue till June 6, 2027.

title: Shubman Gill scripts IPL history, becomes first batter to...
content: Shubman Gill delivered a captain's knock with a blistering 104 off 53 balls, becoming the fastest centurion in IPL playoff history. He also became the first captain to score a playoff century and the first to achieve multiple playoff hundreds. Gill, alongside Sai Sudharsan, forged a record-breaking 160-run partnership, propelling Gujarat Titans to victory.

title: AAP wins big in Punjab civic elections ahead of 2027 assembly polls
content: Punjab's ruling AAP secured a significant victory in municipal polls, winning 5 out of 8 municipal corporations and over 950 wards. This win, with assembly elections approaching, is attributed by CM Bhagwant Mann to the government's welfare schemes. Congress and SAD trailed significantly, while independents emerged as a notable force.

title: ‘Out of form and fitness a concern’: Manjrekar blasts Rohit Sharma's ODI selection
content: Sanjay Manjrekar has questioned the BCCI selectors' decision to include Rohit Sharma in the ODI squad over in-form youngsters Yashasvi Jaiswal and Sai Sudharsan. He believes the selectors are failing to prioritize Indian cricket's future, arguing that Jaiswal and Sudharsan have earned their spots with strong performances, while Rohit's form and fitness are questionable.

title: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce face new wedding strain amid growing concerns
content: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce are reportedly facing significant challenges keeping their wedding plans private as leaks continue to emerge. Reports claim the couple has changed venues and dates, with Swift feeling increasingly vulnerable over trust concerns. Strict guest policies and heightened security measures have also drawn attention. Sources say the wedding is being managed with extraordinary precautions due to privacy and safety concerns, making preparations far more complicated than expected.

title: Top US commander meets Cuban military officials amid rising tensions
content: A top US military commander met Cuban military officials near Guantanamo Bay, a rare high-level engagement amid escalating US pressure on Cuba. The meeting occurred as the Trump administration intensified sanctions and rhetoric, despite ongoing, albeit limited, diplomatic contacts. A new contingent of US sailors and Marines is also deploying to the Caribbean.

title: Operation Sheruwali enters Day 7; searches continue in J&K’s Rajouri
content: Security forces are in the seventh day of a relentless hunt for two to three terrorists in Rajouri's dense forests. The joint operation, bolstered by helicopters, drones, and sniffer dogs, intensified with heavy firing and shelling. A terrorist hideout was also discovered and destroyed. Top officers are overseeing the critical anti-terror mission.

title: Major shock! After Sinner, Djokovic knocked out of French Open 2026
content: French Open 2026 witnessed two huge upsets as Novak Djokovic and Jannik Sinner crashed out of the tournament. Teenager Joao Fonseca stunned Djokovic in a five-set thriller after coming back from two sets down. Earlier, World No. 1 Sinner collapsed physically in extreme Paris heat and lost to Juan Manuel Cerundolo, ending his 30-match winning streak and opening up the men’s draw.

title: "India Powerful, Modernising Military With High-End Ops": Top Trump Aide
content: "India is powerful and modernising its military," Pete Hegseth said. He also highlighted that it is maintaining a balance of power, particularly in the Indian Ocean.

title: No Red Lines, No Deal: Trump's Iran Meet Ends Without "Final Determination"
content: Trump had earlier said in a post on Truth Social that Iran will clear mines it laid in the Strait of Hormuz, US will lift its naval blockade and ships will start moving.

title: 64% Indian Women Now Use Internet, Up From 33% In 2019-21: National Survey
content: The NFHS-5 for the 2019-2021 period had recorded that 33.3% Indian women had used the internet at least once. This figure has doubled over the interim two-year period

title: Iran War LIVE Updates: Trump's Meet With Aides Ends Without "Final Determination"
content: US Iran War LIVE Updates: US President Donald Trump had on Wednesday dismissed an Iranian state media report that Iran and Oman would jointly manage shipping through the Strait of Hormuz

title: "Never Seen Anything Like It": Satellite Pics Show China Is Building Launch Pads Near Nuclear Missile Silos
content: Satellite images show Beijing is building launch pads, bunkers and communications nodes near the isolated nuclear silos that hold the Chinese military's longest-range missiles.

title: Vice Admiral Ajay Kochhar Takes Charge As 48th Vice Chief Of Naval Staff
content: An alumnus of the prestigious National Defence Academy, Pune, Vice Admiral Ajay Kochhar was commissioned into the Indian Navy on July 1, 1988.

title: RBI's Currency Makeover: Why Polymer Notes Are Important
content: Unlike paper currency, polymer notes are more durable and resistant to dirt and moisture, factors that are especially relevant in India's climate and usage conditions.

title: Weather Today LIVE: Temperatures Dip In Many States, Weather Body Flags Below-Normal Monsoon
content: Weather Updates Today: Delhi remained under cloudy skies, logging its lowest maximum temperature in past 21 days. The IMD has forecast rain and thunderstorms across parts of north India, including Punjab, Himachal Pradesh, UP, Uttarakhand.

title: Karnataka Politics Live Updates: Key Congress Meet Today On Cabinet Reshuffle
content: Karnataka Politics Live Updates: A change in leadership in the three-year-old Karnataka government appears imminent, with Siddaramaiah having sought an appointment with Governor Thaawarchand Gehlot, reportedly to tender his resignation.

title: Big Congress Meet On How Many Deputy Chief Ministers Karnataka Will Get
content: The Congress leadership is said to be quite keen on making way for new and young faces in the Karnataka cabinet

title: Supreme Court Flags Rise In Frivolous Criminal Cases In Matrimonial Disputes
content: The observations on the rising trend of filing vexatious litigation were made by a bench in a judgement by which it quashed a criminal complaint alleging that a 14-year-old girl was raped by her father and uncle.

title: 5-Month Planning, Stabbing, Burnt Car: How A Minor Killed 4 Family Members
content: What initially appeared to be an accident involving a burnt car has now turned into one of Rajasthan's most chilling murder investigations.

title: Trump's Name Must Be Removed From Kennedy Center: US Court
content: A US judge on Friday ruled that the famed Kennedy Center for the Performing Arts had been illegally renamed after President Donald Trump and that his name must be taken down.

title: "Trade Priority, Expect More Meetings": US On India Deal
content: India and the United States are on the verge of concluding a highly anticipated interim trade agreement, with the deal expected to be signed within the coming weeks or months.

title: IMF, World Bank, Others Warn Middle East War Is Straining Energy Supplies
content: The US-Israel war on Iran has disrupted trade, rattled financial markets and raised concerns over global energy supplies, particularly through the Strait of Hormuz, a key route for oil and gas shipments.

title: 18-Year-Old Student Dies In School Swimming Pool In Punjab: Cops
content: According to the deceased student's mother, Sunita, her son Aryan had gone to participate in a swimming competition organised at the school.

title: Ex-US Attorney Refused Questions On Trump's Role In Epstein Probe: Democrats
content: In a closed-door interview before the House of Representatives Oversight Committee on Friday, Bondi also said Todd Blanche, who now serves as acting attorney general, had been responsible for the documents' release. Eps

title: Iran Preparing "Grand' Funeral For Ex-Supreme Leader Ayatollah Khamenei: Report
content: Khamenei, who led the Islamic republic for more than three decades, was killed in the first wave of US-Israeli strikes that launched the war on February 28.

title: 66% of births in 2024 were first-borns; birth spacing for majority now 36 months
content: nearly two-thirds of all live births in india - 66.4% - were first-born children . the fourth child and births thereafter dwindled to barely 3.5% . nearly 23% births were second-order births, a term used to refer to second child .

title: 'Maligning isn't constructive criticism': HC on Madhu Kishwar's bail plea
content: a u.s. prosecutor submitted the anticipatory bail plea . a video allegedly resembling the holder of a constitutional post was uploaded and circulated on social media . there is a difference between constructive criticism and tweeting to malign, justice says .

title: Shangri-La Dialogue: India's defence secretary reaffirms military coop with US
content: the defence ministry said that defence secretary Rajesh Kumar Singh engaged with leading think tanks and academia on strengthening the Indo-Pacific security architecture . Singh shared his vision for regional security and strategic engagement . the defence secretary held discussions with the commander of the united states INDOPACOM (indo-Pacific Command)

title: 60% chance of deficient monsoon as IMD downgrades rain forecast
content: Vishwa Mohan is Senior Editor at The Times of India. He writes on environment, climate change, agriculture, water resources and clean energy, tracking policy issues and climate diplomacy. He has been covering Parliament since 2003 to see how politics shaped up domestic policy and India’s position at global platform. Before switching over to explore sustainable development issues, Vishwa had covered internal security and investigative agencies for more than a decade. Read More

title: HCs must pronounce judgment within 3 months of reserving verdict: SC
content: supreme court mandates a three month deadline for HCs to pronounce their verdicts . orders in bail cases must be passed immediately, a bench says . a series of appeals against convictions in criminal cases languishing indefinitely .

title: Karnataka : Fresh power tussle over deputy CM posts, cabinet berths & KPCC chief
content: Manu Aiyappa has been with The Times of India for over two decades, currently serving as the Political Editor. His reporting focuses on politics, public policy, and in-depth features that aim to inform and engage a broad readership. Over the years, his work has been recognised by both the Press Club of Bangalore and the Karnataka Media Academy for its clarity, credibility, and relevance to public discourse. Read More

title: CBI chief vows logical end to NEET probe, NTA says computer-based test will curb leaks
content: the national testing agency said it is taking measures to rule out a repeat of the paper leak . the parliamentary committee on govt assurances said it will work to keep the admission seat matrix for counselling on schedule so that students are not further impacted . committee's agenda pertained to the paper irregularities in 2024 .

title: Only 1% work left on India-US trade deal: US ambassador Sergio Gor

title: Rise in women misusing law to get lucrative settlements: SC
content: the supreme court flagged a "worrying trend" of women misusing laws on dowry harassment, domestic violence and Pocso . the court said genuine cases were getting overshadowed and obscured by the "overwhelming" number of false cases filed by spouses as an "arm-twisting" method to reach a more lucrative settlement .
